required_fields.evidence: present but not strongly typed in protocol; observed as string[] in some records and string in others across ledgers

consensus_schema.validation: Only protocol.json and the launcher prompt define the contract. I found no code that validates consensus ledger rows for required fields, types, or allowed enum values before or after append.

required_fields.evidence: present but not strongly typed in protocol; observed as array in some rows and omitted/inconsistent elsewhere

observed_optional_fields.consensus_item_id: string link to a single consensus record

broadcast_schema.validation: Same as consensus: append-only ledger exists, but there is no runtime schema/type checker, routing validator, or adoption receipt validator.

findings.consensus_logic: The intended workflow is propose -> peer_review -> improve -> consensus -> broadcast -> adopt. In practice, consensus is not computed by code. I found peer_review rows with verdicts like approve/endorse and improvement rows with based_on links, but no vote counter, quorum, threshold, or tie-break algorithm. A commander promotes an item to consensus by writing a new consensus row that references prior items (for example via based_on) and adds its own verdict/tier language.
findings.distribution: Broadcasts are distributed through one shared append-only file: .stampede/{run_id}/collab/broadcasts.jsonl. The launcher exports the same collab paths to every commander, and the prompt tells commanders to consume broadcasts.jsonl in real time. Distribution is therefore fan-out by shared ledger visibility, not targeted delivery: any commander with access to the run directory can read every broadcast. I found no recipient list, subscription model, ack tracking, or per-commander delivery confirmation.
adoption_blockers: Contract is underspecified: protocol.json names only common required fields, not per-event optional fields, types, or enums.
Observed schema drift: evidence is not strongly typed, consensus uses based_on while broadcasts vary between consensus_item_id and adopted_items.
Consensus rules are social/manual, not executable; no quorum or threshold makes outcomes subjective and harder to automate.
Broadcast delivery is passive shared-file reading; there is no recipient targeting, acknowledgement, or adoption receipt ledger.
The launcher prompt is effectively the canonical spec; project docs outside runtime artifacts do not fully document the collaboration bus contract.
findings.evidence_quality: Moderate. The bus has good traceability primitives: append-only JSONL ledgers, stable item_id guidance, source_refs, target_item_id/based_on links, and monitor line counts for proposals/reviews/improvements/consensus/broadcasts. But observability is shallow: the monitor only counts rows, not validity or adoption; I found no parser that enforces malformed-line tolerance for collab ledgers despite protocol guidance, and no system record showing which commanders actually consumed or adopted a broadcast beyond optional bundle references.
